meaning: adj.被关闭的,郁积的 vbl.pen的过去式及过去分词
examples: She burst into tears, releasing all her pent-up emotions.
Years of pent-up ambitions were being unleashed.
The pent-up demand to spoil the grandkids that was frustrated in 2008-09 is still bubbling.
This overall price increase is being made possible, in part, by pent up demand.
